morning.title: Good morning from the little study burrow.
morning.subtitle: Open a note and let the day start softly.

morning.title: Mornings are brighter with you :)
morning.subtitle: Start with one small page and settle in.

morning.title: Good morning, cozy thinker.
morning.subtitle: Your notes are tucked away and ready.

morning.title: The burrow is awake.
morning.subtitle: Pick a note and warm up the brain gently.

morning.title: Sun-Kissed Mornings.
morning.subtitle: A little reading is enough to begin.

morning.title: 404? Imma make some coffee and check back in a bit.
morning.subtitle: The notes will be here when you return.

morning.title: Why did the horse go to the bar?
morning.subtitle: Because it wanted to get away from the barn.

morning.title: Otrovert
morning.subtitle: A person who is both introverted and extroverted, Idk why I thought of this but it made me laugh.

morning.title: Arcane
morning.subtitle: Secret, mysterious, known or understood by only a few.

morning.title: Welcome to 2006
morning.subtitle: AI? COVID? You must have hit your head really hard.

morning.title: Morning News Time *bbc radio music*
morning.subtitle: British Broadcasting Corporation?

morning.title: Good Morning....I have a question for you.
morning.subtitle: Did you name your daughter alexis?

morning.title: Morning has padded in quietly.
morning.subtitle: Your notes are exactly where you left them.

morning.title: Good morning, notes under lamplight.
morning.subtitle: Start gently and let the rhythm build.

morning.title: The study corner is warm.
morning.subtitle: Pick one note and make it a little clearer.

morning.title: Good morning, calm little scholar.
morning.subtitle: Today begins with one simple note.

morning.title: Morning pages.
morning.subtitle: Read a little and let it settle.

morning.title: Good morning, burrow brain.
morning.subtitle: Wake up slowly with a familiar topic.

morning.title: A small note for a fresh morning.
morning.subtitle: No rush, just a gentle first step.

morning.title: Good morning, welcome back in.
morning.subtitle: Your study nook kept your place.

afternoon.title: Helios climbs to his throne.
afternoon.subtitle: The gods do not rest at noon. Nor shall you.

afternoon.title: Thus it is written on the stone of Delphi.
afternoon.subtitle: He who opens his scroll in the afternoon hours shall not be forgotten.

afternoon.title: Hear this, traveller of the afternoon.
afternoon.subtitle: Apollo watches the idle hand with great disappointment.

afternoon.title: The Oracle has spoken.
afternoon.subtitle: Return to your notes before the shadow moves.

afternoon.title: On this wall it is carved for all to see.
afternoon.subtitle: One page read in the heat of day is worth three read by moonlight.

afternoon.title: Behold, the sun stands at its peak.
afternoon.subtitle: This is a sign. Open your scrolls.

afternoon.title: The philosophers of Athens did not nap.
afternoon.subtitle: They debated. You may simply take one note.

afternoon.title: Inscribed by the hand of Thales.
afternoon.subtitle: Know thyself. Start with your last unread note.

afternoon.title: A proclamation to those who wander the agora.
afternoon.subtitle: The afternoon is short. The knowledge is long.

afternoon.title: Zeus himself decreed it.
afternoon.subtitle: The midday scholar shall inherit the evening's clarity.

afternoon.title: It is said by those who carved before us.
afternoon.subtitle: The mind sharpens best when the sun is high.

afternoon.title: Hear ye, citizen of the afternoon hours.
afternoon.subtitle: One clear thought carved now outlasts a thousand left to drift.

afternoon.title: The Stoics left this message on the wall.
afternoon.subtitle: Discipline is the gate. Your note is the key.

afternoon.title: From the steps of the Parthenon, it was declared.
afternoon.subtitle: No hour is wasted if a single idea is understood.

afternoon.title: Athena blessed the afternoon learner.
afternoon.subtitle: She asks only that you return to your page.

afternoon.title: So it is written in the hall of records.
afternoon.subtitle: The scholar who studies at noon does not fear the evening test.

afternoon.title: A warning etched in old marble.
afternoon.subtitle: Time passes whether you read or do not read.

afternoon.title: This stone remembers every afternoon squandered.
afternoon.subtitle: Do not add yours to the count.

afternoon.title: The agora grows loud. Step away from it.
afternoon.subtitle: Your scroll waits in silence and requires only you.

afternoon.title: As Socrates once asked a passerby.
afternoon.subtitle: What do you know, and when did you last check?

evening.title: Helios descends. The sky turns amber.
evening.subtitle: What you learn now shall burn through the night.

evening.title: It is written above the temple gate.
evening.subtitle: The evening scholar walks lighter into the dark.

evening.title: The gods have dimmed the torch of day.
evening.subtitle: Light your own by opening a single page.

evening.title: Carved here by a student of Aristotle.
evening.subtitle: The evening is not the end. It is the quiet part of the work.

evening.title: Heed the inscription on the eastern wall.
evening.subtitle: One idea reviewed at dusk is fixed into the stone of memory.

evening.title: Dusk has arrived as the Fates promised it would.
evening.subtitle: Use what remains of the light wisely.

evening.title: The stars prepare to be your witnesses.
evening.subtitle: Show them a mind still at work.

evening.title: Thus spake the elder at the city wall.
evening.subtitle: The evening hour forgives slowness. It does not forgive stillness.

evening.title: Nyx approaches on quiet feet.
evening.subtitle: Finish one thought before she closes the page.

evening.title: Carved in the stone of the evening temple.
evening.subtitle: What is reviewed before dark is remembered past it.

evening.title: Hermes, messenger of the gods, leaves a note.
evening.subtitle: He says: open your scroll. He has already moved on.

evening.title: The inscription beneath the setting sun reads this.
evening.subtitle: An evening not spent in thought is merely a long afternoon.

evening.title: Let it be known to all who walk this portico.
evening.subtitle: The mind that rests too early rises empty.

evening.title: The Oracle saw you choosing rest over revision.
evening.subtitle: She said nothing. But she wrote it down.

evening.title: Even Diogenes read in the evening.
evening.subtitle: He had no lamp and no excuses. You have both.

evening.title: On this stone the city agrees.
evening.subtitle: One quiet hour of study is worth more than a loud hour of intention.

evening.title: The twilight hour is a gift from Apollon.
evening.subtitle: It asks only that you do not waste it.

evening.title: So it is decreed at the evening assembly.
evening.subtitle: Pick one note. Let it be enough.

evening.title: The last light holds on as long as it can.
evening.subtitle: Meet it halfway. Open something.

evening.title: These words were left here for you specifically.
evening.subtitle: The ancients knew you would be here at this hour, unsure. Begin anyway.

night.title: Nyx has drawn her curtain across the sky.
night.subtitle: Read lightly. The night is not the time for conquest.

night.title: By the light of a single flame, it was carved.
night.subtitle: One small page at night is a gift to the morning self.

night.title: The stars are inscribed above. Your notes are inscribed below.
night.subtitle: Both will outlast tonight.

night.title: This is the proclamation of the night watch.
night.subtitle: Study gently or do not study at all.

night.title: Hypnos waits at the door with patience.
night.subtitle: He will let you finish one note first.

night.title: Written beneath the moon by an unknown hand.
night.subtitle: What you review tonight, you will own by morning.

night.title: The night temple is open but quiet.
night.subtitle: Enter softly and take only what you need.

night.title: Even the gods sleep.
night.subtitle: But first they close their scrolls properly.

night.title: The inscription here is worn but legible.
night.subtitle: Do not let the hour become the reason you stopped.

night.title: It is the decree of the night council.
night.subtitle: One completed thought is a worthy end to any day.

night.title: The torches are low in the hall of learning.
night.subtitle: That is no reason to leave empty-handed.

night.title: Morpheus sends this message ahead of his visit.
night.subtitle: Finish the page. He will wait.

night.title: On the wall beside the sleeping quarters it reads.
night.subtitle: A mind at rest having learned something is a mind at peace.

night.title: The midnight scholar is mentioned in the old texts.
night.subtitle: Not by name. By what they chose to do with the quiet.

night.title: Carved here by lamplight, long ago.
night.subtitle: The night asks little. Give it one clear thought.

night.title: Athena does not sleep, but she understands rest.
night.subtitle: Close the scroll when the words begin to blur.

night.title: This is the final inscription on the night wall.
night.subtitle: What you did today is already written. Add one good line.

night.title: The agora is empty. The scrolls are still full.
night.subtitle: You have the night to yourself. Use a little of it.

night.title: Thus ends the public decree of the evening.
night.subtitle: Rest is permitted. Completion is preferred.

night.title: The gods have noted your presence at this late hour.
night.subtitle: They ask only that you read one page before you go.
